Module com.example.typinggame
Package com.example.typinggame.model

Class Score

java.lang.Object
com.example.typinggame.model.Score
All Implemented Interfaces:
IScore
public class Score extends Object implements IScore
Manages the game's scoring system, tracking points, errors, seasons, and chapters. This class utilizes JavaFX properties to enable reactive UI updates and data binding.

  • Property Summary

    Properties
    Type
    Property
    Description
    javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty
    chapter
    Returns the observable property for the current chapter.
    javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty
    points
    Returns the observable property for the points.
    javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty
    season
    Returns the observable property for the current season.

    • getPoints

      public int getPoints()
      Retrieves the current numerical value of the player's points.
      Specified by:
      getPoints in interface IScore
      Returns:
      The current score as a primitive integer.

    • addPoint

      public void addPoint()
      Increases the player's score by 100 points for a correct answer.
      Specified by:
      addPoint in interface IScore

    • addError

      public void addError()
      Increments the error count and subtracts 50 points from the score. Implements a safety floor: once the player reaches 50 points, the score will not drop below this value due to errors.
      Specified by:
      addError in interface IScore

    • nextStep

      public void nextStep(int currentListSeason)
      Updates the game progress (chapters and seasons). If the season increases, the chapter resets to 1. Otherwise, the chapter increments until reaching the 5th chapter limit.
      Specified by:
      nextStep in interface IScore
      Parameters:
      currentListSeason - The current level/season provided by the word engine.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets all scoring, errors, and progress indicators to their starting values.
      Specified by:
      reset in interface IScore
